title: Beyond Grief
key_takeaway: Do not grieve for what is not worth grieving.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 2.11: Do not grieve for what is not worth grieving.
lifeHack: Do not grieve for what is not worth grieving.

title: The Indestructible
key_takeaway: The soul pervades everything and cannot be destroyed.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 2.17: The soul pervades everything and cannot be destroyed.
lifeHack: The soul pervades everything and cannot be destroyed.

title: The Steady Lamp
key_takeaway: A concentrated mind is like a windless lamp.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 6.19: A concentrated mind is like a windless lamp.
lifeHack: A concentrated mind is like a windless lamp.

title: State of Trance
key_takeaway: In meditation, the mind finds ultimate joy.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 6.20: In meditation, the mind finds ultimate joy.
lifeHack: In meditation, the mind finds ultimate joy.

title: The Gentle Return
key_takeaway: Gently bring the wandering mind back.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 6.26: Gently bring the wandering mind back.
lifeHack: Gently bring the wandering mind back.

title: Crossing Maya
key_takeaway: Divine illusion is hard to cross without surrender.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 7.14: Divine illusion is hard to cross without surrender.
lifeHack: Divine illusion is hard to cross without surrender.

title: Qualities of Knowledge
key_takeaway: Humility and nonviolence are true knowledge.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 13.7-8: Humility and nonviolence are true knowledge.
lifeHack: Humility and nonviolence are true knowledge.

title: Causes of Action
key_takeaway: Every action has five root causes.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 18.13: Every action has five root causes.
lifeHack: Every action has five root causes.

title: Work as Worship
key_takeaway: Worship the divine through your own daily work.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 18.46: Worship the divine through your own daily work.
lifeHack: Worship the divine through your own daily work.

title: The Ego and the Doer
key_takeaway: The bewildered spirit soul, under the influence of the three modes, thinks himself the doer.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 3.27: The bewildered spirit soul, under the influence of the three modes, thinks himself the doer.
lifeHack: The bewildered spirit soul, under the influence of the three modes, thinks himself the doer.

title: The Three Gunas
key_takeaway: Material nature consists of three modes: goodness, passion, and ignorance.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 14.5: Material nature consists of three modes: goodness, passion, and ignorance.
lifeHack: Material nature consists of three modes: goodness, passion, and ignorance.

title: Results of the Gunas
key_takeaway: From goodness develops real knowledge; from passion develops greed.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 14.17: From goodness develops real knowledge; from passion develops greed.
lifeHack: From goodness develops real knowledge; from passion develops greed.

title: Like a Lotus Leaf
key_takeaway: One who performs his duty without attachment is like a lotus leaf untouched by water.
seo_description: Bhagavad Gita 5.10: One who performs his duty without attachment is like a lotus leaf untouched by water.
lifeHack: One who performs his duty without attachment is like a lotus leaf untouched by water.
